title: prunus padus var. cornuta
intro: Prunus padus var. cornuta, also known as Cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, distinguished by its bright red berries and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Prunus padus var. cornuta include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
